pattern: Map each index to a label by testing divisibility, ordering the combined-divisor case first so it is not shadowed by the single-divisor cases.
hints: Walk the numbers from 1 to n and decide each entry independently based on what it is divisible by.
Two separate divisibility tests, by 3 and by 5, partition every number into four buckets; the trick is handling the both-divisible bucket.
Check divisibility by 15 (or by both 3 and 5) first, then by 3, then by 5, and otherwise emit the number as a string.

edge_inputs.description: smallest input, no Fizz or Buzz yet
14 stops one short of FizzBuzz, so the last entry is the literal 14
30 has two FizzBuzz entries (15 and 30), catching solutions that only special-case 15

stress.description: large contiguous range exercising every bucket many times
stress.note: single integer input; size scales the upper bound
